topic: Fabric init CLI UX and bootstrap consolidation

findings.finding: Public install flows for workflow-oriented CLI tools tend to separate detection, selection, installation, and next-step guidance instead of printing only after-the-fact file writes.
findings.detail: The user comparison against Claude Code Workflow / ccw install is directionally consistent with common modern CLI installer patterns even though local repo evidence remains the primary source.

findings.finding: Claude Code Workflow documentation explicitly distinguishes additional Codex setup from the main workflow install path.
findings.detail: This supports the conclusion that if Codex needs extra setup, Fabric init should surface that as a first-class outcome instead of leaving it implicit.

best_practices.rationale: Users are more comfortable with installer CLIs when they can see detected targets, planned writes, and optional components before execution.
best_practices.source: Installer CLI pattern synthesis from public workflow tool documentation

best_practices.practice: Single source of truth plus thin client adapters
best_practices.rationale: Cross-client systems are easier to maintain when native client files become small pointers to a shared internal protocol source.
best_practices.source: Inference from current Fabric bootstrap fanout and prior internal analysis

best_practices.practice: Capability reporting over silent partial success
best_practices.rationale: If a client receives MCP config but not a higher-level skill, the installer should state that explicitly.
best_practices.source: Comparison between Fabric Claude flow and Codex partial flow

alternatives.option: Keep external files but make them thin shims
cons: Still leaves some external files present

pitfalls.issue: Treating all clients as if they support the same post-init protocol
pitfalls.mitigation: Model client capabilities explicitly and report unsupported or manual steps.
pitfalls.source: Comparison of Fabric Claude tests and Codex config tests

pitfalls.issue: Moving everything into .fabric at once without keeping shims
pitfalls.mitigation: Migrate in phases and preserve client-native discovery files as lightweight pointers first.

codebase_gaps.gap: No preflight interactive wizard layer in init
codebase_gaps.current_approach: Linear execution with stage summaries after actions

codebase_gaps.gap: No single-source bootstrap protocol document under .fabric
codebase_gaps.current_approach: Multiple full or semi-full client-facing bootstrap files
codebase_gaps.recommended_approach: Move full protocol text under .fabric and reduce client files to shims

codebase_gaps.gap: No Codex skill parity or explicit missing-skill reporting
codebase_gaps.current_approach: Codex gets MCP config and bootstrap header only
codebase_gaps.recommended_approach: Add capability matrix and eventual skill/manual-step support

sources.title: Claude Code Workflow repository
sources.key_takeaway: Workflow CLIs with multiple clients document client-specific setup gaps explicitly, which aligns with the need for Fabric to surface Codex incompleteness.
